title: Understanding mortgages
description: Practice understanding mortgages: 30 year fixed, 15 year fixed, and ARM

assessment_items.question_content:
There are many mortgage types, but a $30$-year fixed mortgage is the most popular one.

**What is a key characteristic of a $30$-year fixed mortgage?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The interest rate changes annually after the first five years.
You pay back the home loan in $15$ years.
Your mortgage payment is the same every month for as long as you have the loan.
It requires the biggest down payment compared to others.

assessment_items.question_content:
There are many mortgage types, but a $30$-year fixed mortgage is the most popular one.

**Which of the following is a benefit of a $30$-year fixed mortgage program?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The mortgage is paid off in less time compared to most other mortgages.
The interest rate and monthly payments can adjust every year.
The monthly mortgage payments are generally lower than other mortgage types.
The loan requires a smaller down payment than other mortgage types.

assessment_items.question_content:
There are many mortgage types, but a $30$-year fixed mortgage is the most popular one.

**Which of the following is a drawback of a $30$-year fixed mortgage?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The loan has to be paid off in $15$ years.
The interest rate usually goes up after $5$ years.
Over the life of the loan, you end up paying more in total than with shorter mortgages.
The initial down payment is typically higher compared to other loan types.

assessment_items.question_content:
When it comes to fixed loans, the $15$-year and the $30$-year mortgage are the most common choices.

**Which of the following is true about $15$-year fixed mortgage?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: After $15$ years, the interest rate will usually get higher.
You can pay off your home loan in $15$ years.
Its monthly payments are typically *less* than those of other mortgages.
Its total cost over the life of the loan is higher compared to other mortgages.

assessment_items.question_content:
When it comes to fixed loans, the $15$-year and the $30$-year mortgage are the most common choices.

**What is a benefit of a $15$-year fixed mortgage?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It often has a lower interest rate than longer term loans.
Its monthly payments are generally lower than with other mortgage types.
After $15$ years, the monthly payments become much lower.
The loan period will adjust after $7$ years.

assessment_items.question_content:
When it comes to fixed loans, the $15$-year and the $30$-year mortgage are the most common choices.

**Which of the following is a drawback of a $15$-year fixed mortgage?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It has a bigger down payment requirement than other loans.
The monthly payments are typically higher than with a $30$-year mortgage.
The interest rate regularly goes up after $15$ years.
It is more limited in availability than other types of mortgages.

assessment_items.question_content:
ARM (Adjustable Rate Mortgage) loans typically have two numbers as part of their name. These numbers have a meaning.

**In a 5/6 ARM loan, what does the $6$ represent?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The loan lasts for $6$ years total.
The interest rate changes every $6$ years.
The interest rate can change $2$ times a year after the first $5$ years.
The same interest rate lasts for the first $6$ years of the loan.

assessment_items.question_content:
ARM (Adjustable Rate Mortgage) loans are another option to consider when selecting a home loan.

**Which of these describes an Adjustable Rate Mortgage (ARM)?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The first few years have a lower interest rate.
Your payment is the same every month.
You have to pay off the loan in $5$ years.
The interest rate changes every year from the start.

assessment_items.question_content:
You have a 7/1 ARM mortgage.

**How long before the interest rate can start changing?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: In an ARM loan name (like 7/1 ARM), both numbers have a meaning.
The first number refers to the length of time the interest rate remains fixed.
The second number refers to how often the rate changes after that.
In a 7/1 ARM loan, the first number is $7$, so the interest rate will start changing after **$7$ years**.

choices.content: after $1$ year
after $5$ years
after $7$ years
after $10$ years

assessment_items.question_content:
It is important that your choice of mortgage loans matches  your financial goals.

**Which one of these loans would help you pay off your mortgage in the shortest amount of time?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: 5/1 ARM
$15$-year fixed mortgage
$30$-year fixed mortgage

assessment_items.question_content:
It is important that your choice of mortgage loans matches  your financial goals.

**Which of these home loans will take the longest to pay off?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: 5/1 ARM
$15$-year fixed mortgage
$30$-year fixed mortgage

assessment_items.question_content:
It is important that your choice of mortgage loans matches  your financial goals.

**Which of these will have the lowest initial payment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: 5/1 ARM
$15$-year fixed mortgage
$30$-year fixed mortgage
